mnemonics: Indicates separation, to split something
origin: 八 represents the original meaning "to separate or distinguish" using two separated strokes. It was borrowed for its pronunciation (i.e. via sound loan) to represent the number eight.

mnemonics: person is holding or covering something
usage: grouping or enclosing smth (derived from 包)
origin: 勹 depicts a person bending over, as if to lean forward over and rest their weight on an object. The original meaning is to lean forward over and rest one's weight on an object.

mnemonics: cowrie shells were used as currency in ancient times
two halves of a sea shell
animal that crawls in and out of it's shell
origin: 貝 is a picture of a cowry shell. Many ancient cultures used shells as currency. 贝 replaces 貝 in Simplified Chinese via regularization of cursive. In some characters that use the radical 貝 in the traditional form, 贝 is also used for simplification.

mnemonics: possibly a reduction of 仌 [bīng] (ice, cold)
origin: 冫 depicts metal plates. The original meaning is related to 呂 [lǚ] (two copper plates). This meaning can still be seen in 冶 [yě] "to smelt" and 凝 [níng] "to condense". Later it was borrowed to mean "ice".

mnemonics: grass are small plants growing/sprouting side by side
usage: broad term, general concept of plants
origin: 艸 is composed of two 屮 [chè] (non-tree vegetation) components. The character originally represented newly sprouted vegetation (same as 木 [mù] and 屮 [chè]) and only later excluded trees. It is mostly used in component form 艹.

mnemonics: Sprouts growing from the ground (like herbs)
origin: 屮 is a picture of newly sprouted vegetation. Before, this included both smaller, non-tree plants as well as trees (屮 [chè] , 艸 [cǎo] , and 木 [mù] wer used interchangeably). Later, it came to mean only non-tree vegetation.

english: lower form of animal life ( insects, insect larvae, worms)
mnemonics: mosquito planting it's stinger 中 in your revealed skin
origin: 虫 depicts a venomous snake, which is also its original meaning. 虫 was the original character for 虺 [huǐ], but was used (as a component) to write "insect" (traditional: 蟲) as early as the Qin Dynasty. In Simplified Chinese, 虫 replaces 蟲 by omitting two of the three 虫.

measure_word: a trad. unit of length (尺/市尺=10寸/市寸,  市寸=3.3cm=1.3in)
mnemonics: A thing 丶 which is smaller than the hand. The spot one inch below the wrist where the pulse is measured.
usage: actions done by hand (variant of 又)
origin: 寸 depicts a hand with a mark below the wrist, around the area where the pulse is taken, which is also the place where ancient Chinese medicine practitioner cuts cut the veins. Originally the character referred to that spot on the wrist.

mnemonics: A man with arms and legs spread, trying to convey something large
usage: smth. big, large, strong, old, etc.
origin: 大 depicts the front view of a standing adult person. Originally, the character meant adult man, as in opposition to 子 [zǐ], and by extension to also mean big. Later, the character for adult man 夫 [fū] was derived from 大.

mnemonics: a chinese cleaver (squared) knife that is held horizontally
usage: actions undertaken with sharp items like cutting
origin: 刀 is a picture of a knife. The lower/right part is the handle, and the upper/left one is the blade. When it appears as a component on the right side of a character, it is written 刂. Note: In ancient times, there was an old coin shaped like a knife (刀币 [dāobì]), which was based on a bronze tool (削 [xiāo]). This brought the meaning of money to the character of 刀 (US Dollar 美刀 [měidāo]).

components: 臼 [xìn]: empty: corruption of 囟
儿 [rén]: form: a person's body (人)
mnemonics: two legs of a person; child with up-raised hands wanting to be picked up
origin: 儿 depicts the side view of a person standing. It has two different meanings stemming from different origins (兒 [ér] and 人 [rén]). The traditional form 兒 is composed of 臼, a corruption of 囟, and 儿. The sinplified character is also 儿, which is a modern stand-alone. When used as component, 儿 changes its meaning due to being a variant of 人 [rén].

dict_entries: 一二三四五六七八九十
mnemonics: two things
origin: 二 depicts the original meaning "two" via two single horizontal lines, similar to 一 [yī].

mnemonics: mirror image that shows opposite forms; knuckles pressed together, ready to oppose everyone in their way
origin: 非 depicts two people with their backs to each other. The original meaning is to go against or oppose.

mnemonics: Represents steps coming down from a city, or possiby a wall
origin: 阜 is an abbreviation of 𨸏, which is a depiction of hills, indicating the original meaning of a hill. The component form 阝 only appears on the left side of a character.

mnemonics: sheild
origin: 干 depicts and originally means a kind of instrument used for hunting or for military purposes. 干 is also used to abbreviate the characters 乾 [gān] and 幹 [gàn] in Simplified Chinese.

mnemonics: house on a hill; a high building
origin: 高 depicts a tall building, hence the original meaning of tall or lofty.
It looks like the suzhou numeral one

mnemonics: represents a sheath of grain with a sagging head
origin: 禾 is a picture of a millet plant with its ears drooping under the weight of its mature grain. The original meaning is therefore grain or millet.

mnemonics: a fire with sparks
usage: fire and actions related to fire like cooking
origin: 火 is a picture of fire, hence the original meaning of fire or flame. As a component on the bottom of a character, it is often written 灬.

mnemonics: a table
origin: 几 depicts a table. In simplified Chinese it replaces 幾 [jǐ]

mnemonics: a man with an eye for the head; a person looking through a window
usage: attirbutes and actions attributed to perception, seeing, and inspection
origin: 见 depicts a person (儿) with an enlarged eye (originally 目, now simplified), emphasizing the original meaning to see. 见 replaces 見 in Simplified Chinese.
The character shares the same form as 冂 [táng] (hall) in the modern script.

mnemonics: primitive of an open vessel or receptacle
origin: 凵 depicts a sunken-in pit or cave.

measure_word: things with mouths (family members, domestic animals, cannons, wells, etc)
mnemonics: an open/smiling mouth, where food and words enter and get out
usage: things done with one's mouth: speaking, eating, drinking, etc.
origin: 口 depicts a person's open mouth.

english: administrative unit between the township 镇 [zhèn] and neighborhood 邻 [lín]
measure_word: (ancient) measurement of length  (= 500 m = 150 zhang (市丈)
mnemonics: in our neighborhood there are rice fields 田 and a cemetery 土
origin: 里 is composed of a field (田) and a pile of earth (土), indicating the original meaning "a place to live". A place that can be farmed is suitable for living. The character 里 is used in Simplified Chinese to replace 裡 [li] and 裏 [lǐ].

mnemonics: image of a man 人 flexing his muscles
a plow
origin: 力 is a picture of a plow.
茻 probably means numerous grass or weeds. In ancient character forms, the components 茻 and 艹 were used interchangeably dud to similar meaning.

english: entrance (of a building, vehicle or vessel)
mnemonics: a pair of swinging doors
origin: 门 is an abbreviated form of 門. It depicts a double-sided door.

mnemonics: rice growing in a paddie, the horizontal line is the water line
origin: 米 depicts grains of rice.

radical: aka 秃宝盖 [tūbǎogài] or 平宝盖 [píngbǎogài]

mnemonics: The dot on top may represent a chimney, the edges eaves.
usage: things related to houses and buildings
origin: 宀 is a picture of a house, which is the original meaning. In comparison to 广 [yǎn], this hous probably has all four walls.

mnemonics: a willow tree
origin: 木 depicts a tree with the upper part resembling a branch and the lower part resembling a root.

mnemonics: an eye with iris/pupil, topic is something that catches the eye
usage: eyes and what you use them for (look, inspect)
origin: 目 depicts a human eye. As a component, it is often written 罒. However, 罒 has another origin as the component form of 网 [wǎng].

mnemonics: looks like a woman sitting down with legs crossed
usage: having to do with people in general
origin: 女 is a picture of the side view of a woman in a kneeling-sit position. It is unclear whether the ancient character shows the woman's arms folded over her chest or her breasts. The original meaning is woman.

mnemonics: a man blowing air from his mouth, the spirit is floating over the water like steam
usage: gas (as opposed to a liquid or solid)
origin: The ancient character for a cloud is 气. Originally, 氣 used sound loan from 气, while its meaning of giving or serving food was connected to the other compound 米 [mǐ]. Later, the character 氣 was replaced by 餼 [xì], and 气 was replaced by 氣. In Simplified Chinese, the ancient form of 气 is still used to represent 氣.

mnemonics: person standing wide legged
origin: The ancient Chinese character  for 人 resembles a human standing with his hands hanging down on his side.

mnemonics: The sun tells what time of day it is. Japan is the land of the rising sun.
origin: 日 depicts the sun. The character was originally a circle, but because it's not easy to inscribe on oracle bones, the character may have been changed to a square shape. The dot in 日 is to avoid confusion with 囗.

mnemonics: Flesh cut into pieces. After eating the meat, only the (wishing) bones remain
origin: 肉 is a picture of a piece of meat. The original meaning of the character is the meat of birds and other animals. When it appears as a component on the left-hand side of a character, it is written 񰀷 (looks identical to 月).

mnemonics: A small wedge used to hold a door open by lodging it between the door and the floor
The open door of a tent
origin: 入 depicts a wedge, indicating the original meaning "to enter".

mnemonics: a mountain with three peaks
origin: 山 depicts a mountain.

mnemonics: Trimming a tree with (five) branches to make to grow new buds of life
origin: 生 depicts a plant 屮 sprouting from the ground, hence the  original meaning "to grow, to emerge".  The ground was originally as depicted as a horizontal line 一, and later changed to 土 (after a decorative stroke was added).

components: 艮 [gěn]: empty: 艮 replaced 皀 [bī] showing a grain basket
mnemonics: a good 良 restaurant has a peaked roof 񃹡 (the lower two strokes are chopsticks)
origin: 食 depicts a mouth 亼 [jí] over a bamboo basket used to hold grain - originally 皀 [bī], now replaced by 艮 [gěn] - indicating the original meaning "to eat".

mnemonics: kneeling by a stream of water
usage: actions involving liquids (like pouring, flowing, swimming, etc)
origin: 水 depicts flowing water. As a component, it is more often written 氵or  氺.

german: 6. Tier im chin. Tierkreis - Schlange, Jahr der Schlange
mnemonics: a sanke
origin: 巳 depicts a snake-like reptile.  It was borrowed very early via sound loan as one of the Earthly Branches.
冂 depicts the hall of a building. It is the original form of 堂 [táng].  The character shares the same form as KangXi radical 冂 [jiōng].

mnemonics: a rice paddy,  the field is equally ino four parts
origin: 田 depicts a field with crisscrossed footpaths.

mnemonics: The circle/box represents a wall surrounding something, for example, a house
origin: 囗 depicts the borders of a city.

mnemonics: A desk with ink on it for writing and studying. Signs of culture are writing and literature
origin: 文 depicts a human body with linear, tattoo-like markings on it. The word was originally used to refer to the tattoos painted on a body. Later, it referred to symbols in general and was extended to also mean writing.

mnemonics: depicts the slanted moon, representing evening
origin: 夕 depicts the moon at the time of its rising. The original meaning is when the moon rises at sunset in the evening. The characters 月 [yuè] and 夕 were originally both in the form of a moon, with or without a point in the middle. Later on, a point in the moon shape was used to differentiate between moon (月 with point) and eve (夕 without point).

mnemonics: split 八 and make it small
origin: 小 uses three small strokes to represent small pieces, which some people believe represented sand grains.

mnemonics: The human torso is where the heart is. What touches the heart influences mind and soul
origin: 心 is a picture of a heart. The ancients believed that emotions are born in the heart and that the human mind is also dominated by the heart. When it appears as a component on the left side of a character, it is written 忄. On the bottom, it is sometimes written 񁢉.
言 was originally 舌 [shé] (tongue) with an added mark, possibly indicating movement of the tongue. Its original meaning is "spoken language".

radical: KangXi 53: dotted cliff, house on a cliff
mnemonics: house on a hill with a wide/broad view of numerous things
usage: NOT a standalone character (in Traditional Chinese)
origin: 广 is a picture of a building or a hut. It is a simpler housing than 宀, probably because it is missing a fourth wall (on the right side). In Simplified Chinese, 广  replaces 廣 [guǎng] by omitting 黃 [huáng].

english: pr. yāo for greater clarity when spelling out numbers digit by digit.
dict_entries: 一二三四五六七八九十
mnemonics: one thing
usage: is used as form component (i.e. horizont)
origin: 一 depicts the original meaning "one" via a single horizontal line.

english: second of the ten Heavenly Stems (天干)
german: der zweite Himmelstamm des chinesischen Kalenders
origin: 乙 depicts a small stream of water.

english: and (used in a mixed fraction such as one and three fourths)
mnemonics: right hand fingers were chopped off twice (again and again)
usage: things done by man or with one's hand (similar to 手 [shǒu])
origin: 又 is a picture of a right hand with three fingers. The original meaning is "right hand". Later, the character 又 was borrowed as 再 [zài] or 更 [gèng].

mnemonics: precious objects 丶 of a king 王
origin: 玉 depicts three pieces of jade strung together, hance the original meaning of jade. When it appears as a component on the left-hand side, 玉 is written as 𤣩.

mnemonics: the moon is every month in the same position
origin: 月 depicts the (crescent) moon. Based on the lunar cycle of waxing and waning, the character 月 later came to mean the month of the year in the calendar. Originally,  月 and 夕 [xī] were indistinguishable. Later on, to seperate them into two different characters, a point in the shape of the moon was used to represent 月. The component form is identical to component-only form 񰀷 of 肉 [ròu ] (meat)

mnemonics: A baby with wailing arms and a big head
usage: life and a child's role in the family
origin: 子 depicts a child, which is the original meaning.
